name: Coconut Milk Ragi Appam
description: The recipe uses gentle seasoning and controlled oil so the grain, lentil, and vegetable flavors stay clear and clean. Coconut Milk Ragi Appam brings a distinct structure, seasoning pattern, and grain-legume balance to the table.
cooking_steps: Heat an appachatti over medium heat and grease it with a few drops of oil.
Pour 120 ml batter into the center, lift the pan immediately, and swirl so the batter coats the sides thinly while a thicker pool remains in the middle.
Scatter the vegetable into the center only, cover, and cook on medium-low heat for 2.5 to 3 minutes until the edges lace and the middle looks set but springy.
Do not flip. Remove with a flat spatula and serve hot with tomato chutney.
meal_prep_notes: Appam batter keeps 2 days chilled after fermentation. Cooked appam is best the same day, but it can be reheated in a covered pan for 1 minute over low heat. Ferment the batter overnight and cook fresh for the best edge texture.
